name: B.e.a.
description_short: Member-owned credit union in Bethalto, Illinois offering savings accounts, auto loans, signature loans, and other financial services to employees and their families.
description_long:
BEA Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative operating in Bethalto, Illinois since serving the local community. The organization was originally associated with the school district but explicitly operates independently and is not affiliated with the Bethalto School District or its Board of Education. This distinction is important for transparency regarding governance and operational autonomy.

The credit union offers a range of financial products including savings accounts (share accounts), share certificates, auto loans, signature loans, and other loan products. Members can access traditional banking services typical of credit unions, including loan applications and membership contracts. The organization emphasizes member ownership and democratic governance as core to its cooperative model.

BEA Credit Union distinguishes itself through limited but personalized service hours (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday 3:30-5:30 pm), direct management contact, and explicit openness to immediate family members of current members for membership expansion. The organization maintains a small-town, accessible approach with a physical office location and named management staff (Joe Schoppet, Manager; Lisa Callison, Assistant Manager).

The main caveat is that this is a very small, locally-focused credit union with extremely limited hours of operation. The website provides minimal detail about loan terms, interest rates, or specific membership requirements beyond family eligibility. Prospective members should expect a smaller product range and fewer digital services compared to larger credit unions or banks, and availability is restricted to three specific evenings per week.
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure provides democratic governance and potential for higher savings rates and lower loan rates
Explicitly welcomes immediate family members of current members to expand membership access
Personal relationship-based service with named manager and assistant manager contact
Low-cost financial services model typical of credit unions versus for-profit banks
Offers multiple loan products including auto loans and signature loans beyond basic savings
NCUA insurance protection on share accounts up to $250,000
Independent operation provides local decision-making without corporate oversight
cons: Extremely limited hours of operation (only 3 evenings per week, 2 hours each) restricts member access
Website provides no information on interest rates, loan terms, APRs, or fees
No evidence of online or digital banking services; appears to be in-person only
Very small institution with likely limited loan products and services compared to larger credit unions
Membership eligibility restrictions (employment-based or family relation required) may exclude some consumers
best_for: Local Bethalto-area residents with school district employment ties or family connections
Members seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with a local credit union
Consumers available during evening weekday hours (Mon/Wed/Thu 3:30-5:30 pm)
diagnosis: BEA Credit Union is best for local Bethalto, Illinois residents with direct or family connections to current members who value personalized, small-scale community banking. The primary caveat is the severely limited operational hours (6 hours per week) and minimal online presence, making this impractical for members needing flexible access or digital banking services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: B.e.a. — Credit Union in Bethalto, IL | CreditDoc
meta_description: B.e.a. — NCUA-insured credit union in Bethalto, IL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
